title: Quick check, registrar vs DNS host

questions.prompt: A customer is moving their DNS hosting from their registrar to Cloudflare. Which step happens last in the migration?
choices: Create the new zone in Cloudflare and copy every existing record
Verify the new zone resolves correctly when queried directly
Update the NS delegation at the registrar to point at Cloudflare's nameservers
Confirm the existing zone has no records you missed
questions.explanation: The NS delegation change is the last step because it's what makes the world start reading the new host. Doing it before the new zone is verified means the world reads an incomplete zone.

questions.prompt: Records added at the registrar's DNS panel are read by resolvers even when the NS delegation points at a different DNS host.
questions.explanation: Resolvers only read the zone hosted at the nameservers the NS delegation points at. Records added in another panel are orphaned and ignored. This is the single biggest cause of 'I added the record but it's not working' tickets.
